title: Tapping for the Doubted Intuition
subtitle: An EFT sequence for reconnecting with the inner knowing you keep overriding
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the intuition that gets talked out of itself: the feeling that was right, the sense you overrode, the gut signal drowned in rationalisations. Works through the habit of distrust toward a more reliable relationship with your own knowing.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are aware of a gut sense you keep overriding, or after an instance where you ignored your intuition and it turned out to have been right.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for rebuilding trust in your own intuition. The script works through the habit of overriding inner sense, the conditioning that says gut feelings are unreliable, and moves toward treating intuitive information as data worth taking seriously.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I keep second-guessing my gut and it has cost me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I was taught that feelings aren't facts and I have tried to live by that, I accept that something in me does know things.
Even though I am not sure I can trust my intuition, I am open to listening to it more carefully.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The feeling I talked myself out of.
Side of Eye: The conditioning that says my gut is not reliable.
Under Eye: The times I knew and didn't act on the knowing.
Under Nose: Waiting for someone else's logic to confirm what I already felt.
Chin: The cost of overriding what I sensed.
Collarbone: The habit of distrust toward my own inner knowing.
Under Arm: Something in me is paying attention.
Top of Head: My inner voice has been trying to reach me.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: My intuition is information. I can treat it as data.
Side of Eye: I do not have to follow every gut feeling. But I can listen first.
Under Eye: The times my gut was right are worth counting.
Under Nose: I am building a more trusting relationship with my own knowing.
Chin: My intuition and my reason can both have a voice.
Collarbone: I am allowed to trust myself.
Under Arm: I notice the gut sense. I give it a hearing.
Top of Head: My inner knowing is getting clearer and easier to read.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
